reasoning: The DBnomics series for Lyon average temperature shows a steep seasonal decline from late October into the winter months. In 2025, late October (around Oct 21) experienced an average temperature of approximately ~15.1°C, while by late January 2026 it plummeted to a 6.2°C average. Normally, this strong seasonal trend would imply that the probability of the temperature being higher than the 2025-10-26 reading decreases significantly the further into winter we advance (towards January). However, the system instructions explicitly mandate that uncertainty must increase with the forecast horizon and that probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5. To comply with this prescriptive constraint, I have structured my probabilities to monotonically approach 0.5 as the time gap from 2025-10-26 extends from November to late January, adjusting the base estimates accordingly.
